entry.title: Re: Save public variants in apps
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Is this the information you're looking for?

View and Bookmarks

"As an administrator, you can share your own views and bookmarks and make them available to one or more users. And you can also share views created by other users. To be able to do so, you need to have the SAP_CORE_BC_UI_SHARE_PC (User Interface - View Sharing) business catalog assigned to your role."

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Vacation Post Challenge
entry.content: My wife and I in DEC 2023 at the Pico Island Volcano, Azores, Portugal. An incredible experience about volcanoes and the simplicity of life on this island.

entry.title: Re: uploading routing long text with LSMW object 0170 and...
entry.content:
Hi,

In the TXT_OBJ_DS you have to assign your operations structure/file, and in the TLINE_DS you have to assign your long text structure/file. The structure of the long text must be a lower level of the operations one. The key between the operation and long text file can be MATNR, WERKS_D, PLNAL, PLNFOLGE and VORNR. (You will not find all those fields in the TLINE_DS, but you can use them in your file. This is how SAP can define a key between the files).

1 step: maintain the source structures - long text structure in the lower level of the operations.

2 step: maintain the source fields - both  routing and long text structures must have the key fields informed above.

3 step: assign the operations structure to the TXT_OBJ_DS and assign the long text structure to the TLINE_DS.

4 step: Field mapping and conversion rules - review the fields of TXT_OBJ_DS. I'm using like this:

BEGIN_OF_RECORD: TXT_OBJ_DS = INIT_TXT_OBJ_DS.
RECTY: TXT_OBJ_DS-RECTY = '21'.

ACTTYP: TXT_OBJ_DS-ACTTYP = 'H'.

OBJ:TXT_OBJ_DS-OBJ = 'O'.

PLNAL: TXT_OBJ_DS-PLNAL = '1'.      (This is the Group Counter, in my case is always 1).

PLNFL: TXT_OBJ_DS-PLNFL = routing_op-plnfolge.      (routing_op is the name of my structure)

VORNR: TXT_OBJ_DS-VORNR = routing_op-vornr.

TEXT_FROM: TXT_OBJ_DS-TEXT_FROM = 1.

TEXT_TO:  TXT_OBJ_DS-TEXT_TO = '99'.

Review the fields of TLINE_DS. I'm using like this:

_BEGIN_OF_RECORD_:  TLINE_DS = INIT_TLINE_DS.

RECTY: TLINE_DS-RECTY = '22'.

TDFORMAT: TLINE_DS-TDFORMAT = '*'.           (* = this is a paragraph)

TDLINE:  TLINE_DS-TDLINE = OPLONGTEXT-LTXT.        (OPLONGTEXT is the name of my structure)

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Will my existing bookmarks work or do i have to rediscover them?

Thank you

entry.title: Generate .xsodata service from .hdbview
entry.content:
I'm on an old HANA system. I need to select data from a table, filtering them and exposing through an .xsodata service. Reading online I got that I need to create an .hdbview view and use it in my .xsodata service. So, my stations.hdbview it's:

schema="SAP_NTV_OPINTSYS";
query="SELECT \"STATIONCODE\" \"NAME\" FROM \"SAP_NTV_OPINTSYS\".\"NAV_STATION\" WHERE \"COUNTRYCODE\" = 'IT' AND \"INACTIVE\" = '0' AND \"STATIONCLASS\" = 'T' ";

and I think it's correct because it shows as activated.

The .xsodata I'm writing it's this:

service {
    "<path_to_the_hdbview>::stations" as "stations" keys ("STATIONCODE", "NAME");
}

but I can't activate it, there is some sort of error I don't get.

Is this the correct syntax?

Machine translation:
How to Resolve Without Scrap Field for Routing Import Template

As shown in the figure, in HANA PUBLIC CLOUD, you need to fill in the Scrapping field (Japanese version called bad) (scrap) when you create a routing, there is no field in the import template, and it cannot be updated after the data is moved, is there a good solution?

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Custom Fields in Work Center Formula Parameters
entry.content: Thank you for sharing

entry.title: Re: XPath expression
entry.content:
This should help

 

https://www.data2type.de/en/xml-xslt-xslfo/xslt/xslt-xpath-function-reference/alphabetical-xslt-and-xpath-reference/current-date

 

Example 1 - date with timezone in the function context:

fn:current-date() returns: 2007-07-10+12:00.

If a date value with timezone indication applies in the context of the function call, this value is returned. Here, a timezone indication in the form +12 hours compared to UTC time is included.

Example 2 - date without timezone in the function context:

fn:current-date() returns: 2007-07-10.

If a date value without timezone is valid in the context of the function call, the mere date value is returned. Here – in contrast to xs:dateTime indications – no Z is attached to the value (see fn:current-dateTime(), example 2).

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Workflow for Proficiency Level Changes of an Attribute in Growth Portf

entry.content:
Hi @kingofthe_ring 

It may not be possible with standard API's but you can create Custom API to achieve this.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/post-incoming-payments-with-an-api-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/qaa-p/12728309#M4146447

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: SU01 Parameter Changes Not Reflecting in SAP SSAM App...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Adding a SU01 parameter alone is not enough for SSAM to pick it up. This also needs to be maintained by the functional expert in the MIAF configuration panel(/syclo/ConfigPanel). this needs to be configured for each respective object you want to recive data down for.(work orders , notifications, Equipment etc....) 

Only once it is also maintained here is the SSAM app aware of it and will include this in the data download.  

Kind regards, 
James

entry.title: Not able to extend "My Inbox(F0862)" application for Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working on extending the standard My Inbox app for Purchase Requisition approvals in S/4HANA 2022. The main requirement is to display a list of approvers in the approval detail screen of the app.

I have followed the steps from these SAP Community blogs:
Fiori My Inbox 2.0 – Extend Approve Purchase Order – S/4HANA 1610
Extend My Inbox 2.0 Purchase Requisition S/4 Hana

What I’ve Done:

  • Extended standard CDS views:
    • C_PurRequisitionFs
    • C_PurRequisitionItemFs
    • Added the required fields and defined @ui annotations like UI.LineItem and UI.Identification
  • Verified that the new fields are available in:
    • OData $metadata
    • Annotations preview (/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/catalogservice;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITION_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value)

Issue:

Although the fields appear correctly in the metadata and annotation files, they are not displayed in the UI of the My Inbox app(Purchase Requisition Approval).

What I’ve Verified:

  • CDS annotations are correctly defined
  • OData metadata is refreshed and active

Question:

Has anyone implemented a similar approver list extension for this scenario successfully?

Any suggestions or shared experiences would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ADRNA field (address of departure point)
entry.content: Has this issue been resolved?

entry.title: New Process for Changing Display Names in SAP Community
entry.content:
Currently as an SAP Community member, you maintain your platform display name within the settings section of your profile (https://community.sap.com/t5/user/myprofilepage/tab/personal-profile). With a change coming on Monday, June 16, however, you’ll manage your SAP Community username via your SAP Universal ID account manager.

With this new process, should you wish to update the username displayed on SAP Community, you’ll still have the option to edit via your profile page – accessible from the “My content” selection in the user dropdown menu available in the upper right of the site.

Once within your SAP Community profile, you’ll see a pencil icon for editing usernames next to your display name.

Selecting that icon will direct you to your SAP Universal ID account (https://account.sap.com/), where you’ll have the ability to change the username that appears on SAP Community. Simply sign in to the account manager and click "Edit."

You can then change and save your username for SAP Community.

You can update your username as often as you’d like, although we encourage you to post as yourself – to help build a stronger community. As always, please make sure that your username conforms to our rules of engagement. For example, you should not use your company name, SAP, or your userID as or in your display name.

Thanks & Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Has someone any idea how can I write this complex Query in sql-anywhere?
entry.content:
Here is a rough idea of an implementation.  You may want to handle domains with more detail - I have simply single quoted values whose domains that are character based.

create or replace procedure mytest (in par1 varchar(10) , in par2 varchar(10), in par3 int)
    result (ss long varchar)
begin
    declare @wip long varchar;

    -- build a list of parms with placeholders for input arguments
    select list(
        string(
            parm_name, '=',
            -- single quote character domains
            if( domain_id in (6,7,8,9,10,13,32,33,34,35,36)) then
                ''''
            else
                ''
            end if,
            -- construct a substute placeholder :<parm_id>
            ':', 
            parm_id, 
            -- single quote character domains  
            if( domain_id in (6,7,8,9,10,13,32,33,34,35,36)) then
                ''''
            else
                ''
            end if
    
        ), ', ' order by parm_id) into @wip
    from sysprocparm 
    where 
           proc_id = @@procid 
       and parm_mode_in = 'y';

    begin
        declare @parmid integer;
        declare @parmname char(128);
        declare @parmval long varchar;

        declare parms cursor for
            select parm_id, parm_name from sysprocparm
                where             
                      proc_id = @@procid 
                  and parm_mode_in = 'y'
                order by parm_id;
        open parms with hold;
        loop
            fetch next parms into @parmid, @parmname;
            if sqlcode <> 0 then leave endif;
            -- get the parameter value, handling any NULL parms
            execute immediate 'select cast(' || @parmname || ' as long varchar) into @parmval';
        -- replace placeholder with value
        if @parmval is null then
            set @parmval = 'null';
            set @wip = replace(@wip, string(''':',@parmid,''''), @parmval);    
        else
            set @wip = replace(@wip, string(':',@parmid), @parmval); 
        end if;
        end loop;        
        close parms;
    end;
    
    select @wip;
end;

call mytest('abc',null,3);

entry.title: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definition Not Found" Post Shared Environment Redeploy
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm encountering a frustrating issue with SAP Build Process Automation and its integration with SAP Build Apps, and I'm hoping someone here might have some insights.

I initially deployed my process to the Public Environment and was able to successfully trigger it from an SAP Build App without any problems. However, due to the need to enable notifications, I had to switch to a Shared Environment. After redeploying the same process to this new shared environment, the integration with SAP Build Apps stopped working.

I'm now consistently receiving the following error message when trying to trigger the workflow from my SAP Build App:

 

{"error":{"code":"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.definition.not.found","message":"Workflow definition not found","logId":"xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"}}

This clearly indicates that the SAP Build App can no longer locate the workflow definition in the new environment.

Here's what I've already tried to resolve the issue, without success:

  1. Deployed the process into a brand new project in the Shared Environment.
  2. Verified the deployment status in the Library within SAP Build Process Automation to ensure the process is indeed deployed and active in the Shared Environment.
  3. Reconfigured the integration within the SAP Build App by updating the process and action references to point to the newly deployed process in the Shared Environment.
  4. Created a completely new action within the SAP Build App, linking it to the redeployed process.

Despite these steps, the "Workflow definition not found" error persists. It seems like the SAP Build App is still looking for the old definition in the Public Environment, or perhaps there's a caching issue, or a deeper configuration problem with Shared Environments that I'm overlooking.

Has anyone encountered a similar problem when migrating processes from a Public to a Shared Environment, or when dealing with "Workflow definition not found" errors after a redeployment? Any suggestions or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: How to Read Data In Mass Process/Consolidation Using...
entry.content:
Hi Mines,

You have to use the respective PRC tables if you are using enhancement route.

Another way is to use DQM rule to validate this scenario or use native BRF+ for consolidation.

You can refer to the community page How-to Information for SAP Master Data Governance | SAP Community

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Error with transporting assignment of BP Identificati...
entry.content:
Hello @talke 

Thanks for the update. Suppose the Identification Category is already present in the test system, but the customised transport still won’t be released or imported due to a dependency issue. In that case, it may be that the system hasn’t fully recognised the software collection as “active” from the customising layer's point of view.

In such cases, SAP Support may need to reset the dependency flag or resync the transport layer manually. Since you’ve already raised a ticket, I’d recommend referencing this explicitly and attaching a screenshot of the blocked transport status.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
@mkrieg 

"These 10.000 Euro are now split two part" : each part is a section of the variable pay template, and each section can have a predefined weight applied to it (based on attributes, and not on UserID) --> Whether you apply the weight to the target amount (as VarPay does in standard) or to the final payout is the exact same thing mathematically.

Now back to my question from my last comment "this split differs from employee to employee": this is where you need to put on your Variable Pay implementation consultant hat and say "Wait a minute", are we sure we cannot establish a pattern or a rule here ? Can you give me a report of these splits for the last cycle ?" Could we imagine a situation where next year Manager are forced to chose between a set list with splits like 10/90, 20/80, 30/70, ....all the way to 90/10 ? The reason you are driving change here is because you don't want to have to create one Bonus Plan (where section weights are defined) by employee, it's worst practice design that can severely impact the performance of the module. 

your target is to build a list of bonus plans that look like this : 

Billable 90 / Goals 10

Billable 80 / Goals 20

...

where eligibility is determined based on a field in EC with these exact same values (or if it is in a Performance Form you can build an integration center job to bring to an MDF Object for example)

etc

 

If you are still lost with my explanations on Bonus Plans I'm afraid it's going to be a complicated implementation for you indeed and you should consider getting someone on board to help here because Bonus Plans are pretty fundamental VarPay knowledge.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: BEHAVIOR_STATEMENT_ILLEGAL error using EML for entity I_ServiceOrderTP

This way, our chatbot can ingest the informaiton and include the direct link to the material. Please advise on how to extract this information. Thank you. Charlene

entry.title: SAP CPQ Local Pricing vs Stateless Pricing vs Document Pricing
entry.content:
Introduction

The Document Pricing feature was introduced some time back in 2023 (I think), and with each new CPQ releases the Document Pricing becomes more prevalent - more customers are adopting it for their project, switching over from Stateless Pricing to Document Pricing.

For those who are new to this topic: both the Stateless Pricing and Document Pricing are pricing provided by SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing Service, one might ponder what's the difference between the 2 option since pricing still comes from CPS?

Note: SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing Service is also known as VCP, but I will refer to as CPS (prefers the older name) in this article.

To better understand, let's recap on the following topics in the following order:

  1. Recap - CPQ Local Pricing
  2. Recap - Stateless Pricing
  3. Finally - Stateless Pricing vs Document Pricing

 

Recap - CPQ Local Pricing

CPQ Local Pricing refers to one where prices and/or costs are stored locally in CPQ (Pricebooks and/or Custom Tables), and then have the CPQ Calculation Engine to compute and provide the results. In this section let's briefly explore the capabilities and power of CPQ Calculation Engine.

Item level calculations

On item level, users can give discounts by percent, discounts by amount, unit net price or extended amount (subtotal). In a typical discount scenario, user applies a discount percent of 25, and CPQ computes forward and calculates the discount amount, net price, extended amount - as shown below.

CPQ supports reverse calculations, meaning to say that the calculation engine not only computes forward but also in reversal manner. In the example below instead of applying a discount percent, user applies a discount amount and the calculation engine not only computes forward for the net price and extended amount, it also does a reverse calculation for the discount percent.

In another example, user sets the extended amount for Qty of 2 items, the calculation engine does a reverse calculation for the net price (unit price), discount amount and discount percent.

Header level calculations

Sometimes applying discounts on item level can be cumbersome in the scenario for large Quotes with hundred of items, and here's where CPQ allows user to set header level discounts or Quote total price - the calculation engine does a reverse calculations and pro-rates out the discounts onto the product type and the item level.

In short, whichever way the discounts or prices are applied the numbers (i.e discount percent, discount amount, net price, etc) will always tally up - now that's really sweet isn't it?

 

Recap - Stateless Pricing

In Stateless Pricing, prices are fetched from the CPS pricing service in real-time, no prices are store locally in CPQ.

In standard integration, the CPS pricing response the net value is mapped onto the standard List Price column in CPQ. Net value is the "final" computed amount, after taking into account list price, freight, discounts, surcharges and miscellaneous condition types in a pricing procedure.

Users can choose to apply further discounts to provide a better selling price, and in this case it will leverage on the CPQ calculation engine to compute the discounts and prices. Now one may also wonder: if the item net value has already factored in the discounts in stateless pricing, won't a further discounts in CPQ actually sort of "doubles" it up?

CPQ stores each CPS pricing response on item level (VCPricingPayload or VCItemPricingPayload), to avoid situation such as above i.e. double discounting, one can write a script to iterate the CPS pricing response to extract each pricing condition and have them mapped out onto the desired item fields.

Example script to mapped out each pricing condition to CPQ item fields:

In the example below, instead of using the standard integration which maps the net value to CPQ List Price, the script maps the pricing response list price (PR00) and discounts (ZRA1) onto the respective item fields, and then have CPQ calculation engine derived the net price (which is the pricing response net value). In this way, user have clarity on the various pricing conditions, allowing them to be better equip when applying or even reducing the discounts.

The above may look like a perfect solution but it still uses CPQ calculation engine which differs from how ERP calculates - you will sometimes encounter a scenario where there are 1 cents or 2 cents difference in the amount in CPQ Quote vs ERP Sales Order (there is difference in how each system does the rounding and the number of decimals involve at each step).

 

Finally - Stateless Pricing vs Document Pricing

Document Pricing takes after the ERP calculation technique, the example below illustrates ERP calculation technique: the calculation steps through from step 10 to step 40, if user decides to give a manual fixed discount amount at step 40, it does not do a reverse calculation to determine the discount percent, unlike CPQ Calculation Engine.

When CPQ have the document pricing feature turned on, you will be able to see the list of pricing procedures available from CPS from within CPQ setup - add the desired pricing condition and map the item fields to each pricing condition.

On using document pricing, the item fields are simply "space holders", it doesn't matter if you map PR00 to a standard List Price field or any standard/custom item field, the eventual calculation will all just work! However we usually try to find an equivalent item fields to map, e.g. PR00 to standard List Price item field and not create additional custom item fields unnecessarily. This is also important if you intent to mixed items using variant pricing and CPQ local pricing, then it make more sense to map them appropriately to present the figures correctly on the Quote.

One can also view the price condition details from within CPQ, there's no need to head over to CPS to view them.

 

Conclusions

After going through my article which describes CPQ Local Pricing, Stateless Pricing and Document Pricing, I hope one can better understand the differences and have a better know-how on its use and application.

Speaking of advantages and disadvantages, IMO I don't think one is more superior than the other, e.g. CPQ Local Pricing via CPQ Calculation Engine will always have an edge in performance over pricing from CPS, but you will need to have all pricing conditions replicated and stored locally in CPQ. At the end of the day it really depends on the customer needs, their system architecture as well as future plans.

I hope you enjoy the read and if you're interested in how-to-setup-document-pricing, drop me a comment or DM. Signing off - Kelvin.

entry.title: Re: Lead status auto update based on Qualification level
entry.content:
After taking a peek at the config. it seems this behavior of qualification levels and statuses are hard coded in the standard C4C system. I see there is a scoping question to stop this from happening but there is no further control. 

 

So you might want to check if there is any way to enhance that process through sdk. 

Julius

entry.title: Erro in API POST
entry.content: It's possible to create a sales order with reference to another Billing document with the Sales Order (A2X, OData V2) API?

entry.title: Value Mismatch in Order and Invoice value due delivery split
entry.content:
I have sales order with one line item having 20 Qty later 3 deliveries are created (8 Qty, 8Qty, 4 Qty), in sales order Condition type (Handling cost) having calculation type B-Fixed Amount is entered manually (Let say 95 EUR), Now all three deliveries  are combined in single invoice now system added 95 EUR to each line item (Order Net Value = 100 (Base value) +95 (Handling cost) = 195 EUR, Invoice value 100+95+95+95= 385 EUR).

Business want in invoice also Handling cost should come only once, How to achieve this ?

entry.title: Re: Electronic bank statement processing failed
entry.content: Hi Anita, I found the solution, guided by the following notes: https://me.sap.com/notes/386971 , https://me.sap.com/notes/668271. Adding that SOFFPHIO class doesn't show me the error.

entry.title: Re: x.400 connection support in SAP Integration Suite for...
entry.content:
Hello @alex_bundschuh,

It's a bit frustrating to see that this request is still a long-term planned extension for SAP CPI. Even though I'd like to see x.400 replaced by another solution like AS2 it's still being used heavily by retailers all over Europe. Looking at the names of the companies that voted for this request on https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/250987/?section=sectionVotes it makes me wonder if the development of such a solution could be given higher priority.
At the time we have chosen to switch to CPI we were told that the x.400 solution would come some time in 2024.

Regards,
Mathias

entry.title: Re: Supplier Return Process in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello, 

The purchasing inforecord is main object that combines material with supplier, prices and information that supplier provides about material. It's crucial to have this information stored in system to manage materials in proper way during creating any order. If you have multiple items on PO and you want to return just some of them inforecord helps you to determine data that are required to create such order. 

Please find more information about retur delivery, inforecord and whole process of return in SAP documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/dc4de156ff2cf777e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/e0b7551de37f445aa227ced2c7c375a2.html?locale=en-US

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2502/SolP/BMK?region=DE 

Hope it will help!

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SA...
entry.content:
Hello,

You have to  map all mandatory fields (those with an occurrence: 1..1) and all errors will disappear.

entry.title: Create Billing Plan for a Sales Order Item through a Custom Program
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have a requirement to create Billing Plan (with multiple line items) for a Sales Order Item by passing Billing Date and Billing Value using an excel file. 

I tried using below FMs in the given sequence but am unable to achieve the required results:

1. BILLING_SCHEDULE_GENERATE followed by BILLING_SCHEDULE_SAVE

2. BILLING_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN followed by BILLING_SCHEDULE_SAVE

My observations:

1. BILLING_SCHEDULE_GENERATE and BILLING_SCHEDULE_MAINTAIN was not able to fill required fields like FPLTR, etc. in the FPLT_NEW local table. 

2.  BILLING_SCHEDULE_SAVE directly inserts the database table FPLT with the values passed by the program in FPLT_NEW internal table. This leaves the database table FPLT with empty values in required columns.

3. All the 3 mentioned FMs are not released for customer use.

4. I tried using BDC and it creates the entries as required with all the required columns. This is because the standard VA02 transaction creates/derives the values of the required columns by itself using the standard program.

My concern:

1. Since BDC is not a preferred solution over using standard FMs, I am not sure if this approach should be followed.

2. The FMs are not released for customers and also there is a direct insert on database table FPLT based on the data passed by the custom program which looks incorrect. 

I am looking for your expert advice in this matter. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Recommended Approach for Check Printing in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (SDDW Replacement)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We’re implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and currently use a legacy check writing tool (SDDW) to handle:

  • Physical check printing with MICR lines

  • Positive Pay file uploads to banks

  • Secure assignment of check numbers and tracking of voided/reprinted checks

  • Remittance data formatted within the check layout

We’re now looking to align this process with our SAP Public Cloud deployment.

Does SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offer any native functionality or recommended approaches for handling these requirements?
Are there specific apps, configuration steps, or certified third-party tools commonly used for this?

Appreciate any insights from others who’ve handled similar scenarios or modernized this process.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content:
Hello, 

You manage adresses with Business Patner functions. Each BP has assigned different functions in their master data, you assign these functions to them and you can manage it from there. If their adrresess are different you should change them there. 

Please see some blogs about BP configuration:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/s-4-hana-business-partner-partner-function-vs-partner-relationships/ba-p/13355200

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/sap-s4-hana-business-partner-bp-end-to-end-configurations/ba-p/13550890 

Best regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: I it possible to export the SAP Enable Now extended standard content for easier SME review
entry.content: Hello, Would it be possible for you to share the script? We've been doing it manually, prior to publishing. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Data volume problems during an SAP S/4HANA Conversion
entry.content: Yes @DotEiserman .

entry.title: Re: limitations of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) when it come...
entry.content:
Hi Greg

Thank you for the information. Could you please review below features listed below  and let me know if any corrections are required 

FeatureSAP BOSAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
Dynamic Recipients (Enterprise recipients, user groups, etc.) Not fully supported. SAC supports scheduling to specific users/teams but lacks dynamic recipient logic.
Enterprise Recipients Yes Not supported like BO's enterprise recipient model. SAC is more manual or limited to team/shared structures.
Personalization (e.g. by region, user) Yes (via report bursting and profiles) Limited personalization. Some personalization is possible using input controls or story filters, but not as powerful as BO bursting.
Events-Based Scheduling Yes (schedule based on event triggers) Not supported. SAC currently only supports time-based scheduling.
Publication Prompts Yes Limited. Prompt handling exists for data models (e.g., input parameters or filters), but not for publications in the same way as BO.
Output Format - PDF, Excel Yes Supported (PDF, Excel, PowerPoint formats available for export/scheduling)
HTML Output Format Yes Not supported for scheduled exports. You can view stories live in HTML in the browser, but cannot schedule in HTML format.
Destination: Email Yes, with options like TO, CC, BCC Partially supported. You can send schedules via email, but no fine-grained control over TO/CC/BCC like BO.
Destination: BI Inbox / FTP / File Server Yes Not supported. SAC currently only supports email or SAC file repository.

entry.title: Export SAP EnableNow Metadata
entry.content:
Hello, How can i export a file from SAP EnableNow that include the metadata (e;g., title, link) in an Excel or csv format?  I would like to include the links in our our chatbox.  THANKS -Charlene

@ShaneLipke

entry.content: thanks for your reply. We have tested this already but before going the custom route we want to understand what is the purpose of the CustomerPayment API. Can this be used for this requirement

entry.title: Re: SAC - API - script - story filter - how to add member...
entry.content: I can see that this could be one of the common requests from many customers. Voted. Thank you for raising the awareness

entry.title: F2071 create notification while saving Creating operations are disabled for entity 'EAM_OBJPG_MAINTN
entry.content:
Hi experts,

In F2071 there is a hidden button 'create', we can make it visible via adapt-ui

When we click on 'create' and try to save the notification the following message appears


Creating operations are disabled for entity 'EAM_OBJPG_MAINTNOTIFICATION~C_OBJPGMAINTNOTIFICATI'
Found note 3155352 , but this one is old from 2022.

app details

When we check the /sap/opu/odata/sap/EAM_OBJPG_MAINTNOTIFICATION_SRV/$metadata , and search for 'sap:creatable=',  we see a lot of sap:creatable="false" (336 times)

Does anyone know if it's possible to make it workable? Or is this the reason why the button is hidden?  😁

Thanks in advance

entry.title: I would like to clarify a few questions I have regarding the transport of features in SAP Cloud ALM.
entry.content:
Question:
Is it possible to schedule a job to automate the creation of transport requests?

Question:
Is it possible to filter features based on transport requests?

Question:
Is it possible to assign an Owner to a feature's transport request via SAP Cloud ALM?

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content: Hi Everyone!  My name is Kendall Tyler and I have been in SAP for the last 33 years and Signavio is the first product in a long time that is a game changer, conversation starter and a way to get those ECC Customers on their journey to S/4HANA.  I am the host of the SAP PRESS Book Club and I have 2 interviews YouTube under SAP PRESS, where I interview the authors of the 2 books that came out on Signavio!  Some people tell me that all I talk about is Signavio, but I seem to find a place for it in every conversation. 🙂  I am waiting for a LeanIX and Walkme book to be written.... hint hint...  Cheers, Kendall

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Optimizing Business Rules
entry.content:
Hi, hope someone can help with a lookup function related to effective dated data.

I have a custom MDF object (called Agentes de Salud) contains a field called "Obra Social" and I want to be filled automatically by retrieving the last record from another custom MDF object (called Alta Obra Social). This object has 2 records registered. 

I built an INITIALIZE rule, place a lookup function, but only retrieves the first record. 

this is the rule

the rule fills the field in the target custom MDF object (Agentes de Salud) with the value of the 1st record, I´d like to be filled with the last record in field "Obra Social" = ADOS ROSARIO

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Generate .xsodata service from .hdbview
entry.content: what error are you getting? also.... you should try to create a calc view not an hdbview

entry.title: Work Approval for Multiple work Orders for PTW
entry.content:
hi All,

We are implementing PTW for SAP EAM Phase based methodology. Our client requirement is to assign one PTW for multiple Work orders, as they have daily inspection/ operation at heights or under water for which they do not want to raise PTW everyday for every single order.

Is there a process to create a PTW and keep it open for some days (14 days for example) and extend the same permit on need basis for multiple work orders, instead of creating a new permit for every single work order ? Also, can you please tell how does PTW/WCM work in case of emergency work orders? 

PLM Enterprise Asset Management (EAM)/Plant Maintenance (PM) #Permit to work

entry.title: Setting Up a Unified Joule Instance: Key Considerations and Common Questions
entry.content:
As SAP’s generative AI assistant, Joule is designed to deliver a consistent, AI-powered experience across SAP’s portfolio—spanning applications like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP SuccessFactors, and more. A Unified Joule instance ensures seamless interactions and insights across these systems, improving productivity and decision-making at scale.

To help customers set up Unified Joule efficiently, I’ve created a series of step-by-step missions in the SAP Discovery Center. These missions guide you through the process of configuring a Unified Joule instance tailored to your SAP environment.

Core Discovery Center Missions:

  • Establish a Unified Joule Instance (pre-requisite for enabling Joule across your SAP landscape)
  • Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

While the Discovery Center missions above include detailed guidance to help ensure a smooth Joule setup experience, I still frequently receive questions—particularly around SAP Cloud Identity Services integration.  Setting up Joule correctly requires consistent SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS) integration across your SAP portfolio in alignment with Identity and Access Management reference architectures.

In this section, I’ll address some of the most common questions and highlight key considerations for integrating Cloud Identity Services across your SAP landscape when enabling Joule.

Identity & Security Considerations for Joule Setup

1. Can I use different IAS tenants for different SAP applications and still have a unified Joule experience?

No. For a Unified Joule setup, all production applications must use the same IAS production tenant for authentication. Likewise, all non-production systems must use a shared non-prod IAS tenant. This architecture enables you to maintain one Joule instance for production and another for non-production.

2. My landscape isn’t fully aligned with the reference architecture yet, but there’s pressure from the business to enable Joule. What are my options?

If your applications aren’t yet configured to use a common Cloud Identity Services (IAS) tenant, you can still move forward by setting up individual Joule instances per application.

This allows you to enable Joule for each application independently; however, it does not follow the Unified Joule model. In this setup:

  • Joule can only respond to queries related to the specific application it’s configured with

  • There is added complexity in managing multiple BTP subaccounts and maintaining separate Joule configurations

While this approach can meet short-term business demands, aligning with the reference architecture remains the recommended path for a scalable and integrated experience.

3. My SAP application supports Joule, but it's not yet integrated with IAS for authentication. What should I consider when setting up SAML or OIDC trust with IAS?

When configuring trust between your SAP application and SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) using SAML or OIDC, it’s important to follow SAP’s recommended domain and configuration guidelines to ensure Joule works as expected.

Key considerations:

  • Use the cloud.sap domain for all integration points.
    For example, when configuring SAML, ensure the Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL in your application uses the cloud.sap domain—not ondemand.com.

  • Access your IAS tenant at:
    https://<tenant>.accounts.cloud.sap/admin
    (Avoid using ondemand.com for any trust-related setup.)

  • To retrieve the correct SAML metadata:

    1. Log into your IAS tenant

    2. Navigate to: Application & Resources > Tenant Settings > SAML 2.0 Configuration

    3. Download the metadata file and use it to configure your application's SAML settings

  • For OIDC integration:

    • Go to: Application & Resources > Tenant Settings > OpenID Connect Configuration

    • Use the configuration details provided there to establish the trust relationship

Important: Do not manually change key parameters in IAS (e.g., the issuer). Altering these settings may disrupt existing trust configurations and prevent your application from authenticating properly.

4. I see a blank screen when launching Joule. What’s wrong?

This often points to a misconfigured OIDC trust between BTP subaccount and IAS. Verify that trust is set up with the cloud.sap domain—not ondemand.com.

5. My existing SAP application is configured with SAML trust to IAS using the ondemand.com domain. Can I enable Joule for this application?

Not in its current configuration. To use Joule, your application’s SAML trust must be updated to use the cloud.sap domain.

As outlined earlier, Joule requires trust configurations aligned with the cloud.sap namespace. While SAP is working on an enhancement to simplify this requirement, updating the SAML configuration to use cloud.sap endpoints is currently mandatory for Joule integration.

6. My application uses a corporate Identity Provider for authentication. How should I configure the Joule application in IAS?

When you run the Joule booster in SAP BTP, it automatically creates a corresponding Joule application in IAS (typically named das-ias-<appname>).

To ensure seamless user authentication, you’ll need to update the conditional authentication settings of this Joule application in IAS so they align with the configuration used by your source application (e.g., SuccessFactors, S/4HANA). This ensures that users are routed correctly through your corporate Identity Provider when accessing Joule.

7. My IAS tenant is configured in pure proxy mode with a corporate Identity Provider like Microsoft Entra ID or Okta. Can I still use Joule?

Not in its current form. For Joule to function properly, a user record must exist in the SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) user store, as Joule relies on attributes like the Global User ID (GUID), which are stored there.

If your IAS tenant is set up in pure proxy mode—meaning Identity Federation is disabled—IAS doesn’t pick up the user records and therefore can’t provide the GUID Joule requires. In this configuration, Joule will not work.

To enable Joule:

  • Ensure Identity Federation is enabled in IAS (either tenant-wide or for specific applications)

  • Maintain a user profile in IAS for each user, even if authentication is delegated to a corporate IdP

This hybrid setup allows authentication to flow through your corporate IdP while still enabling IAS to store essential user attributes needed by Joule.

8. When integrating SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) with a corporate Identity Provider (IdP) for use with Joule, what should I keep in mind?

  • Enable Identity Federation: This must be turned on either at the IAS tenant level or for the specific applications that will use Joule.
  • Maintain user records in IAS: Even when using a corporate IdP, user profiles must exist in the IAS user store. Joule relies on fields like the Global User ID (GUID), which are stored in IAS.
  • Correct SAML/OIDC trust setup: Ensure that the trust between IAS and your corporate IdP is configured properly, using the correct domain(cloud.sap) and metadata. Misconfigured trust can prevent Joule from functioning as expected.

For OIDC integration between IAS and corporate IDP,  refer to the blogs below:

Connect SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service as a proxy to Okta using OpenID Connect

Configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services and Microsoft Entra ID for Joule

For SAML integration:

Connect Okta to Identity Authentication

Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services for Single sign-on with Microsoft Entra ID

NOTE: For SAML integration between IAS and your corporate IDP, make sure to get the IAS SAML metadata using the URL: http://<iashost>.accounts.cloud.sap/admin.

9. Can I setup Unified Joule instance if my applications use a different corporate Identity Providers?

If your SAP applications—such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition—are connected to the same IAS tenant but each is integrated with a different corporate Identity Provider (e.g., SuccessFactors with MS Entra ID and S/4HANA with Okta), a Unified Joule instance is not supported.

This is because Joule requires consistent conditional authentication settings in IAS, and those settings must point to a single corporate Identity Provider. Since IAS can’t route authentication to multiple IdPs for the same Joule instance, you’ll need to configure separate Joule instances for each application and tailor the IAS settings accordingly.

10. I don’t see the Identity Provisioning tab in the admin console of SAP Cloud Identity Services. Why?

Two possibilities:

  • Your user lacks the "Manage Identity Provisioning" permission.  Assign this to the user through the admin console of SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Your tenant is still using Neo-based Identity Provisioning, which has a separate admin console

11. Can I use Neo Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) for Joule?

While possible, we recommend migrating to the latest SAP Cloud Identity Services for IPS.  To use Neo IPS tenant, you will need to make sure that SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition connector is available when creating a target system in IPS. 

Benefits of migration:

  • Unified admin console for IAS and IPS
  • Out-of-the-box availability of SAP Build Work Zone connector.
  • Improved logging, visual transformation editor, and job simulation

Learn how to migrate your IPS instance:

Go for your quick win! Migrate Identity Provisioning tenants to SAP Cloud Identity infrastructure.

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/migrate-identity-provisioning-bundle-tenant

12. Why is it necessary to synchronize users from applications like SuccessFactors or S/4HANA into SAP Build Work Zone for Joule?

Joule supports a range of interaction patterns—including informational, transactional, analytical, and navigational use cases.

To enable navigational experiences (e.g., allowing Joule to guide users directly to relevant pages or apps), user data must be synchronized from your source systems (like SuccessFactors or S/4HANA) into the SAP Build Work Zone instance that’s configured for Joule.

Without this synchronization, navigational capabilities won't be available to end users during their interactions with Joule.

13. Joule works for SSO users in SuccessFactors, but not for password-based users. Why?

As a SuccessFactors customer using Joule, you might have a mix of users—most authenticating via SSO through your corporate Identity Provider, and others using password-based login via the IDP-initiated URL. While Joule functions correctly for SSO users, it may not work for password-based users accessing the system through the IDP-initiated login.

To resolve this, configure conditional authentication rules in IAS:

  • Redirect SSO users to your corporate Identity Provider

  • Allow password-based users to authenticate directly with IAS

  • Use the standard SuccessFactors tenant URL for all users (not the IDP-initiated URL)

You can achieve this by grouping users in IAS (e.g., SSO vs. password users) and applying conditional authentication settings to manage the appropriate login paths for SuccessFactors.

Final Thoughts

Establishing a Unified Joule instance requires careful planning around identity, trust configuration, and provisioning. Following the SAP Discovery Center missions and aligning with the Identity and Access Management reference architectures will ensure you unlock the full potential of Joule across your SAP landscape.

If you have more questions or unique scenarios, feel free to leave a comment or reach out directly.

entry.title: Disabled fields during import contact process
entry.content:
Hello,

During the process of import data (Contacts), we have noticed that some fields are disabled, we would like to know if this may be cause by some issue with the file or these fields are disabled by the system?

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disabled fields during import contact process
entry.content:
This field list is only related to the fields allowed to be used as unique identifier for contact imports. By default, that not all fields are enabled to be used as unique identifier, but these fields can be used normally as part of the contact data in the CSV file.

If there is a field that you would like to be enabled to be used as unique identifier, please contact SAP Support and the team will be able to enable the necessary fields.

entry.title: Carry forward commitment with Value type 80
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

We want to carry forward Commitment with Value type 80 Funds block with the budget but we can’t select this kind of commitment in tx FMJ2.

We use update profile 0359 for Spanish customers and we add this Value type in Override Update Profile. Anyway, in SPRO node Enter Settings for Commitment Carry forward Payment Budget we have no option for this Value type.

Could anyone help us with this issue, please?

Thanks in advance.

Valentyna

entry.title: Exploring Copilot Integration with SuccessFactors Recruiting via API
entry.content:
Our team is currently exploring the possibility of integrating Microsoft Copilot with our ATS system, SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting, to streamline the resume screening process through an API connection.

The goal is to enable Copilot to extract key data—such as resumes, job descriptions, and candidate details—from SuccessFactors, analyze the information, and return the best-matched candidates directly to recruiters via Microsoft Teams. This would be a bi-directional integration, allowing data to flow both ways between Copilot and SuccessFactors.

We’re currently looking to exchange the following fields:

From SuccessFactors to Copilot Agent:

  • candidateId
  • applicationId
  • jobRequisitionId
  • firstName, lastName, email
  • resume (base64/pdf/link)
  • currentStatus (e.g., New, In Review)
  • jobTitle, department, location
  • submissionDate
  • source (e.g., LinkedIn, career page)

From Copilot Agent to SuccessFactors:

  • screeningScore
  • screeningSummary or AI Comments
  • updatedStatus (e.g., Shortlisted, Rejected)
  • recommendationFlag (Yes/No)
  • screeningDate
  • audit trail or reasoning log (optional, for compliance)

Question for the community:
Has anyone attempted a similar integration, or can confirm whether this type of bi-directional API connection is feasible with SuccessFactors Recruiting?

Any insights, best practices, or technical guidance would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Country Compliance Forms "Notice of Pay Rate" California WTPA form Field Configuration
entry.content:
Hi,

The CA WTPA Form is only applicable to non-exempt employees. How can we add a filter for this? I don't see WTPA forms available in the "Trigger Compliance Forms" rule scenario. 

Thank you in advance for your response.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Learning
entry.content: Good afternoon Margit, thank you for responding. After some digging around, I was able to get into the areas of the Learning Hub where I was able to track down courses related to BTP. Hi Serigne, thank you again for your assistance. I had to do a 'combo' fix with what you and Margit mentioned on how to resolve. All take care!

entry.title: Re: SAP Inventory Diagnostics for Retail (IDR)
entry.content: Yes, This is product from SAP and it was in the intial stage of development.

entry.title: Re: How to set up an ATC check run for enhancements in an S/4HANA 758 system?
entry.content:
Hi Bärbel,

can you check if you get a result when you check it from Eclipse ADT with Run As -> ABAP Test Cockpit, for example for SAPMV45A or for one of your enhancements?

For me it works already with SAPMV45A and I get both the findings of the modifications and the enhancements in there.

entry.title: Re: New Process for Changing Display Names in SAP Community
entry.content:
Hi Jerry,

after changing the process, you keep your current name or does it change?

Can you give a reason why this process was changed?

entry.title: I am getting 'No data found ' error when ever i am trying to create the object in SAP RAP
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am new to the RAP and I am using the Unmanaged scenario with Draft. When ever I tried to create the object I am getting the No data found error message even though the key field data is filled. Same scenario is working with out draft. Could anyone help me on this issue please. 

Thanks and Regards,

Prasanth.

entry.title: Re: PPF message not always processed, stucked in SPPFP (S...
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you check the SLG1 logs to see for issues.

Also you can schedule a job out of SPPFP to pick any output in yellow and process it through a background job with specific time intervals. You can also stop the queue and ask the ABAP team to analyze the issue.

entry.title: Re: Modern ABAP: Reduce# operator and FOR GROUPS
entry.content:
Unless this is an ECC system, create a CDS view to do that instead of using ABAP.

That aside, every time a blog like this gets posted I wonder why does anyone look at the "after" code and feels it's better than "before" code. Maybe I'm too old for this but I look at FOR GROUPS ... LET ... REDUCE ... FOR... and it's just gibberish. 

The "before" code could be updated to be "leaner", e.g. lv_netpr variable is not needed and it'd look better with descriptive names and no Hungarian Notation. But otherwise just look at the code: in the "before" example it is very clear what it does, even if someone doesn't know ABAP syntax. It reads like natural language. The "after" is a hot mess.

P.S. Also, I really like this cheat sheet for all Modern ABAP needs: https://www.brandeis.de/en/blog/cheat-sheet-modern-abap-en

entry.title: Re: Reusable Logger Class in ABAP – Simple Logging in Reports
entry.content:
Just use ABAP Logger, it's been available for many years: https://github.com/ABAP-Logger/ABAP-Logger 

There are very many open-source ABAP projects these days. I'd recommend to check here every time one has an idea to build something reusable. Chances are, someone already did.

entry.title: Re: Simplest way to get Message Mapping in Excel Sheet
entry.content:
Hello Rashmi, 

I am trying to but i am now stuck at point 6

  1. yes i can extract the values in the tag and put in a notepad and save it as say a.zip.
  2. This is invalid zip as the zip application cannot open it
  3. I decode then it gives all gibberish and when i encode it is back to base 64

so i do decode or encode via the link that you have given?

Then do i copy the values after the decode OR encode into a new notepad and save it again as b.zip?

But this is invalid zip as the zip application cannot open it

Could you please explain from 6 - 10 step in first method?

I have tried NWDS but it seems that if the mapping is complex or has UDF or something like that it does not export. I have exported the simpler mappings

entry.title: Re: How to Trigger an Error Message Using the Condition P...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can add a requirement routine in the pricing procedure against the gross price and try to do a validation with the PR price and populate an error message.

entry.title: Capturing logged on username in Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm pretty sure this is not possible, but maybe someone out there has a way.

We are trying to personalize scoresheets in test Mode by putting the logged on username in there. For example, would look something like "Well done JOHN SMITH...You've successfully completed this course". I've tried using placeholders, but nothing...

entry.title: Re: Deploying a Fiori Adaption Project application to the...
entry.content:
Hello @Puffblende 

If you're in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and can’t find apps (despite having all available roles), here are the most common reasons and how to troubleshoot:

1. Check if the App Is Assigned to a Business Catalogue

Even if you have all roles, apps are only visible if the underlying business catalogues are assigned to your user.

  • Go to App Finder > check if the missing app appears under "All Apps".
  • If not, use App “Maintain Business Roles” (or app ID F2814) to
    • Open your assigned business role.
    • Go to the Business Catalogs tab.
    • Check if the catalog containing the missing app is present.

You can search SAP Fiori Apps Library to see which catalog an app belongs to.

2. App May Be Part of a Scope Item Not Yet Activated

Some apps are only available once a related scope item is activated in CBC.

Check

  • Use the “Manage Your Solution” or “Configure Your Solution” app.
  • Look for relevant scope items (e.g., 1GA, 2I2, 3EJ, etc.).
  • If the related functionality isn’t active, the apps won’t appear even if the catalogue is assigned

3.Launchpad Content Not Refreshed

If roles or catalogues were recently updated, your Launchpad cache may not be refreshed.

Try:

  • Logging out and back in.
  • Or use “Clear User Personalisation” from the user menu, if available.

4. App Might Be Hidden or Deprecated

Apps can be

  • Renamed or replaced by newer ones
  • Not visible due to SAP standard role restrictions, even if “all roles” are assigned

Use the Fiori App Reference Library to confirm:

  • App ID
  • Required scope item
  • Catalog
  • Restrictions or prerequisites

5.Check Identity and Access Settings

Use the "Maintain Business Users" app:

  • Confirm your user is assigned to the right business roles (not just technical roles)
  • Each role should be published and active

Let me know the outcome

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SA...
entry.content: Thank you Andrej 🙂 it helped. Just 1 question - i can post a new question but maybe you know already. I know that there is an option for uploading a message mapping in the package. I can use NWDS to export message mapping as xlsx but only zip format can be uploaded in the package -> add -> message mapping. Is there a way to convert the exported message mappings to zip? i have been searching and i am unable to find any post on this. And anyways complex mappings and those with UDF are not getting exported in NWDS so that is another issue. Any advice?

entry.title: Re: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SA...
entry.content: Hi Andrej, i have 2 target messages and when i add them (adding target messages.png) they look ok but when i say ok and come back to the actual mapping screen it shows additional IDOC as target in amber colour even though message 1 is email and message 2 is already an IDOC. Reference - adding idoc.png. I need only 1 target IDOC and i cannot get rid of this last target under the top node Messages (extra idoc under messages.png)

entry.title: Re: Story reports showing time values one hour prior to actual time values
entry.content: Hello! I'm facing the same issue, only that I have a 3-hour difference. I've tried everything but nothing seems to work. Have you found any solution for this inconvenience? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: I am getting 'No data found ' error when ever i am tr...
entry.content:
Hello @Prasanth13 

The “No data found” error in your RAP unmanaged with draft setup is likely caused by the system’s inability to read draft data properly.

Key reasons:

  1. Missing READ_DRAFT_ENTITY method
    – You must implement this to return draft data. Without it, the UI can’t find the draft.
  2. GET_DRAFT_ADMIN_DATA not implemented
    – Needed to return draft metadata like DraftUUID, timestamps, etc.
  3. Incorrect handling of IS_ACTIVE_ENTITY
    The framework checks if it's a draft or active record. If your read logic doesn’t handle IS_ACTIVE_ENTITY = abap_false, no draft data will show.
  4. Draft tables not filled
    – On creation, make sure draft data is written correctly to your draft table.

What to do:

  • Implement READ_DRAFT_ENTITY
  • Implement GET_DRAFT_ADMIN_DATA
  • Handle IS_ACTIVE_ENTITY properly
  • Check that draft records are written

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Tutorials no longer open
entry.content:
Hello @LJ1010 

If the tutorials on the SAP Build Apps tutorial page aren't opening, regardless of the browser, here are a few possible causes and quick steps to resolve the issue:

Common Causes and solutions

1.Pop-up Blockers or Browser Extensions

Some tutorials open in overlays or new tabs.

  • Disable any ad blockers, privacy extensions, or script blockers
  • Allow pop-ups for developers.sap.com in your browser settings

2.Cookies & Local Storage Issues

Old or corrupted cache/cookies can block interactive content.

  • Open the page in private/incognito mode
  • Or clear cache & cookies for developers.sap.com

3.SAP Tutorial Platform Glitch

The content may not load due to internal issues with SAP’s tutorial platform.

  • Open the direct link to the tutorial:
    https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-create.html
  • If that doesn’t work, test another tutorial from a different category (e.g. CAP, BTP onboarding) to see if it's site-wide

4.Network / Firewall Issues

Corporate or school networks might block SAP content domains.

Try this

  • Switch to a personal or mobile hotspot network temporarily
  • Check browser console (F12 > Console tab) for blocked scripts or CORS errors

If none of this works, let me know what region you’re accessing from or if you’re behind a company VPN

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Near Fixed bin Strategy : SAP-EWM
entry.content: Thanks for explaining very clearly rohela

entry.title: Re: Next Generation - IT Operating Model (SAP Centric)
entry.content: hey @JustinBroughton , thanks for the article, very insightful.

entry.title: Re: ✨ How to Identify SAP Fiori Apps Based on RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model)
entry.content:
This post clearly has been written with ChatGPT. Please check the Rules of Engagement for the new rules about GenAI assisted content: https://pages.community.sap.com/resources/rules-of-engagement

Please be aware that these types of posts are unlikely to impress anyone in a professional community. People are quite good at seeing through the generated content and it doesn't help to build a good reputation. So, why do it?

entry.title: Manage SAP Build projects as part of your enterprise architecture
entry.content:
Recently, I transitioned from SAP Landscape Management to the product management team of SAP LeanIX. I landed in a fantastic team already hard at work on the integration between SAP LeanIX and SAP Build, and I was fortunate enough to help bring the first release across the finish line, which was announced last week.

Now, you can seamlessly connect your SAP Build instances with your SAP LeanIX workspaces, enabling automatic discovery of your SAP Build projects. This empowers enterprise architects to model these projects as application fact sheets, integrating them directly into their daily workflows within SAP LeanIX.

SAP Build is described as a "unified application development and process automation solution, enabling personalization and efficiency at scale." (source) It's a vital part of our customers' IT landscapes, complementing their existing SAP solutions. Consequently, the custom projects setup within SAP Build represent the unique, differentiating elements of their landscapes. Managing these projects alongside other SAP and non-SAP solutions within SAP LeanIX is a natural and essential next step.

Connecting the Dots

Rather than diving deep into the documentation, let me give you a quick overview of how to regularly discover your SAP Build projects within your SAP LeanIX workspace.

You'll need an instance of SAP Build Process Automation (even if you primarily use other SAP Build components) and an instance of SAP Cloud Management Service within your SAP BTP subaccount. The Cloud Management Service provides the context to the SAP BTP Global and Subaccount. Using service keys for these two instances, you can configure the new "SAP Build" integration within the admin area of SAP LeanIX.  Once you complete the onboarding wizard, your SAP Build projects will be automatically discovered and regularly updated.

The SAP Discovery inbox will present you with a list of all discovered projects. From there, you can link them to existing fact sheets or create new ones. The integration intelligently suggests the following fact sheet types:

  • Application: The project itself
  • IT Component: The technology used within the project (e.g. SAP Build Code)
  • Platform: SAP BTP, your global account, and the subaccount
  • Provider: SAP

I'm eager to hear your thoughts on this new integration! Does this address a pain point in your enterprise architecture work? What other challenges are you facing when managing extensions to your SAP solution portfolio?  Share your feedback in the comments below or on our roadmap item!

entry.title: Manual Tax entry between two partner companies
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have created a manual tax entry between two partner companies, however, when checking the Journal Entry, the Partner company ID is not filled.
Why is it happening?

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Manual Tax entry between two partner companies
entry.content:
Dear All,

The Business Partner field in Manual Tax Entries is not posting relevant. It is only used for reporting purposes. 

Refer to the KBA 3434365 - Business Partner Field in Manual Tax Entries not Derived in Journal Entry https://me.sap.com/notes/3434365 for additional details, please.

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
Hello Sascha,

same problem for me. 

Did you check the log in /var/tmp/hdb_HDB_hdblcm_register_rename_system_*/hdbrename.log

On my side, the hdblcm want to change permissions on a folder which is not present:

22:36:13.425 - INFO:   Creating temporary Local Secure Store file system
22:36:13.425 - ERR :     Failed to change permissions of directory '/usr/sap/HDB/lss'

I have tried a lot. But nothing helps. I assume, that it is a bug in the Appliance.
I am currently open a SAP Call.

Best regards

Steffen Herrmann

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Vinay,

The most recent information that is available on this topic is in Kristina's blog post that was released last week.  I would definitely subscribe to that to stay informed on this topic.

Regards
Shane
